laws.title: Public Law 2-11 | Criminal Statute of Limitations Act
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this law as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence, so the law is not current despite the substantive limitations rule in the source text.

provisions.text_summary: Sets a 75-day limitations period for most Title 18 crimes, a 150-day period for listed chapters, and measures accrual from the time of the offense.
provisions.exact_change: No operative code change; preserve as repealed history only.
provisions.evidence: "75 days"; "150 days"; "commence at the time the offense takes place"
provisions.notes: The source text itself is real, but the final legal status is repealed.

current_implementation.detail: The working tree carries a statutory-note stub with the source dump and archive link, but the law is not current and should remain history only.

recommended_actions: Keep only a historical statutory note.
Do not enact the statute-of-limitations language as operative Code text.
Mark the law repealed/nonoperative in the audit record.

laws.title: Public Law 2-12 | Fee Prohibition Act
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this law as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence.

provisions.text_summary: Bars courts in the United States, including the Supreme Court, from imposing or collecting fees unless the charge is punishment for a criminal offense.
provisions.exact_change: No operative code change; preserve as repealed history only.
provisions.evidence: "No Court in the United States, including the Supreme Court, shall impose, collect, or otherwise require fees"
provisions.notes: Substantive fee-ban language, but not current law.

current_implementation.detail: The live XML keeps the law as a note stub in Title 50, but the final legal status is repealed so it should stay historical.

recommended_actions: Retain as historical note only.
Do not move the fee ban into operative Title 28 text.
Mark the law repealed/nonoperative in the audit record.

laws.title: Public Law 2-13 | The Employees Termination Notification Act of 2021
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this law as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence.

provisions.text_summary: Requires a terminating federal-agency or private-business employer to tell the employee the reason for termination within 48 hours.
provisions.exact_change: No operative code change; preserve as repealed history only.
provisions.evidence: "notify an employee of his/her termination with the reason"; "forty-eight hours (48 hours)"
provisions.notes: The source text creates a real notice rule, but it is not current law.

current_implementation.detail: The working tree parks the law as a note in Title 5, but the disposition is repealed so no operative labor-text change is warranted.

recommended_actions: Keep only a historical note.
Do not codify the 48-hour notice rule as current text.
Mark the law repealed/nonoperative in the audit record.

laws.title: Public Law 2-14 | National Federal Torts Act
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this law as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence.

provisions.text_summary: Contains the short title, effective date, conflict clause, and severability language.
provisions.exact_change: No operative code change; preserve as repealed history only.
provisions.evidence: "National Federal Torts Act"; "immediately upon passage"; "shall be severable"

provisions.text_summary: Creates federal-court jurisdiction, enumerates tort causes of action, and supplies government-liability and false-arrest remedies.
provisions.exact_change: No operative code change; preserve as repealed history only.
provisions.evidence: "civil lawsuit in the United States District Court"; "TRESPASSING"; "FALSE ARREST"; "systematic rights violation"
provisions.notes: The source text is broad, but the final legal status is repealed.

current_implementation.detail: The working tree note is a placeholder history stub in Title 28 territory; the law remains repealed and should not be treated as active tort code.

recommended_actions: Retain as historical note only.
Do not codify the tort/jurisdiction framework as operative Title 28 text.
Mark the law repealed/nonoperative in the audit record.

laws.title: Public Law 3-16 | The Capitol Police Establishment
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this law as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence.

provisions.text_summary: Creates the Capitol Police Board and vests it with appointment, removal, and security-regulation authority.
provisions.exact_change: No operative code change; preserve as repealed history only.
provisions.evidence: "The following shall be members of the Capitol Police Board"; "Appoint a Capitol Police Chief"; "regulate the security of the Capitol Buildings"

provisions.text_summary: Authorizes protection, warrantless arrests in protective duties, Capitol-complex policing, and outside-jurisdiction deployment limits.
provisions.exact_change: No operative code change; preserve as repealed history only.
provisions.evidence: "make arrests without warrant"; "police the United States Capitol Buildings and Grounds"; "may not deploy any officer outside"
provisions.notes: The law is structurally substantive, but it is not current.

current_implementation.detail: The working tree keeps the law as a note stub in Title 18 territory, but the final legal status is repealed so no current Capitol Police code is authorized from this law.

recommended_actions: Retain only a historical note.
Do not codify the Capitol Police framework as operative Title 2 text.
Mark the law repealed/nonoperative in the audit record.

laws.title: Public Law 3-17 | The Diplomatic Protections Act
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this law as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence.

provisions.text_summary: Defines foreign diplomats, requires protection, authorizes removal and deportation, grants immunity when authorized, and sets a 24-hour departure timetable.
provisions.exact_change: No operative code change; preserve as repealed history only.
provisions.evidence: "Foreign Diplomat"; "provide adequate protection"; "Diplomatic Immunity"; "24hr timetable to leave the United States"

current_implementation.detail: The live XML note is in Title 22 territory, but the law is repealed and should remain history only.

recommended_actions: Keep only a historical note.
Do not codify the diplomatic-protection rules as current text.
Mark the law repealed/nonoperative in the audit record.

provisions.evidence: "Section V and its entirely shall be disbanded"; "Capitol Police Outside Prescribed Jurisdiction"

laws.title: Public Law 3-19 | The NARA Act

provisions.text_summary: Requires the President to notify the Archivist of the United States of all bills signed.

provisions.evidence: "notify the Archivist of the United States of all Bills he so signs"
provisions.notes: This is the one law in the batch that remains active.

current_implementation.detail: The live XML note is in the proper records-management title and matches the current statutory-note disposition.

recommended_actions: Keep the statutory note at 2 U.S.C. section 6506.
Do not add extra operative text beyond the note placement.
Leave the law marked active / statutory-note.

laws.title: Public Law 3-20 | National Immigration Employment Act of 2021

provisions.text_summary: The source PDF has no extractable text, so the operative provisions cannot be reconstructed from the batch materials.
provisions.exact_change: No operative code change; preserve as source-limited history only.
provisions.notes: The disposition is still repealed, but provision-level auditing is source-limited.
